Empires & Puzzles Rustbeak Review: 3-Star Holy Hero Guide
Don't let the cute feathers fool you. This 3-star Construct is a mechanical menace in disguise. We analyze why Rustbeak might just be the best tank for your 3-star tournament defense team.
Rustbeak: The Steampunk Owl Who Saved the Summon

Rustbeak is a 3-star Holy (Yellow) hero from the Construct family, introduced in April 2024. In a portal often dominated by flashy 5-stars, this little mechanical owl managed to steal the show for many players. He brings a potent combination of healing, dodge, and critical chance buffing to the 3-star arena, a rarity level where such complex utility is scarce. He operates as a support tank or flank, designed to keep his team alive while frustrating the enemy with evasive maneuvers.

The artwork for Rustbeak is a delightful mix of nature and machinery, fitting perfectly with the Construct family theme. He appears to be a mechanical owl with brass and copper plating, gears visible in his wings, yet retaining the piercing gaze of a predator. It's a steampunk ornithologist's dream. The animation is crisp, and let's be honest, who doesn't want a robot owl on their team? It's definitely a hoot.

Rustbeak boasts a Power of 495, which is exceptionally high for a 3-star hero, significantly eclipsing older Classic heroes who sit around the 450 mark. His stats are well-balanced but lean slightly towards survivability with
. When Limit Broken and emblemed, these numbers skyrocket, making him incredibly sturdy for his rarity. He is built to take a hit and keep ticking.
Swooping Support is a triple-threat defensive skill.
429 HP over 3 turns is a substantial amount for 3-star heroes, whose health pools are smaller.
The +45% chance to dodge Special Skills
attacks is the crown jewel here. Dodge is one of the strongest defensive mechanics in the game, and having it on a 3-star at Average speed is game-changing.
The +45% Critical Chance is a nice offensive cherry on top, helping your team punch through enemy defenses with tile damage.
The limitation is that it applies only to the "Caster and nearby allies." However, this is mitigated by his Construct Core passive (see below).
Let's break down the math behind the mechanical bird.
Average (10 tiles to charge). In Rush Attack tournaments, this becomes Very Fast (6.5 tiles), making him significantly more dangerous.
429 HP / 3 turns = 143 HP per turn for 3 heroes. Total potential HoT: 1287 HP.
allies when fully charged. For a team with avg 1000 HP, that's an instant 250 HP heal to everyone.
Mitigating 45% of incoming damage sources (Special + Tiles) effectively nearly doubles the survivability of the protected heroes against those sources for the duration.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family brings a unique "charging" mechanic that adds a layer of depth to Rustbeak's gameplay.
While Rustbeak doesn't have a traditional stat-boosting family bonus like some others, the Construct Core is the defining feature.
This is what makes Rustbeak truly elite. While his special skill only heals nearby allies, his passive heals

allies by up to 25% boosted health when the core is fully charged. Since he is likely to be taking hits (charging the core) as a tank or flank, he will often fire with a significant charge, providing a massive burst heal to the whole team in addition to his HoT.
A fantastic passive for a healer/support. It means heroes like Proteus or other mana blockers in the 3-star arena (if any future ones arise) won't easily shut him down.
at the start of battle is top-tier for a support hero. It protects him from early defense down or mana control ailments, ensuring he can fire his first special and get the Dodge train rolling.
Released in April 2024, Rustbeak entered the game when 3-star power creep was accelerating with Toon costumes and other event heroes.

His raw stats (495 power) and the complexity of his kit (Passive Heal + Active HoT + Dodge + Crit) put him comfortably ahead of older 3-stars. He represents the "new era" of 3-stars that feel like mini-5-stars.
Rustbeak is a staple for competitive 3-star play. He is not just a collector's item; he is a meta contender for 3-star Buff Booster and Rush Attack tournaments.
Rustbeak currently does not have a costume.
As a Druid, Rustbeak has access to the Companion talent (chance to summon a Thorn Minion). This adds a small meat shield, further increasing survivability.
Maximizes his ability to survive as a Tank or Flank. The longer he lives, the more he heals and dodges. His healing is flat (HoT) or percentage-based (Core), so high Attack isn't strictly necessary.
Increases tile damage and the Thorn Minion's health/attack.
Makes him squishier, which is counter-intuitive for his role.
path is recommended because Rustbeak's primary job is to stay alive, protect the team, and keep the healing flowing.
Dodge + HoT + Core Heal + sturdy stats make him very hard to kill.
The Repair Core patches the "nearby only" weakness of his special.
One of the best defensive buffs, rare in 3-star rarity.
Protects him during the crucial opening turns.
If the core isn't charged, his active skill only protects 3 heroes.
As a buffer, he hates dispellers like Tyrum or Sabina (if playing mixed rarity).
To get the full team heal, he needs to take hits to charge the core.
He provides 2 buffs (Dodge, Crit), fueling the rule perfectly.
Firing at Very Fast speed makes the Dodge uptime nearly permanent.
Great for keeping the team alive in difficult Challenge Event stages restricted to Rare heroes.
Rustbeak shines as a Tank or Flank where he can absorb tiles to charge his Core.
Rustbeak protects the center while Kvasir adds minion buffers.
A Rush Attack nightmare. Rustbeak adds Dodge to Faiez's ailment immunity.
Tyrum (Costume), Belith (Costume), and Mnesseus remove his Dodge and HoT.
Heroes that prevent buffs can neuter his special skill entirely.
If you pull Rustbeak, level him immediately. He will be a cornerstone of your 3-star tournament defense and offense teams for a long time. He is much more valuable than most Season 1 3-stars.

Don't overlook him just because he's a 3-star. In the competitive world of 3-star tournaments, Rustbeak is a top-tier asset. He is worth the Limit Break and even the Alpha Aether if you are serious about tournament loot.
The community reaction to Rustbeak was overwhelmingly positive, especially compared to the 4-star in the same portal.
"Rustbeak, you have saved this portal honestly this little three star owl has completely saved this portal."
- A popular sentiment from early reviews.
"He is swan maiden without the drawback of not allowing buffs on his teammates."
- Forum user comparing him to a 5-star mechanic.
"Has tank potential, he is meant to annoy the enemies."

Rustbeak is a prime example of a modern 3-star hero done right. He brings mechanics usually reserved for Legendary heroes down to the Rare level without breaking the game—but definitely bending it in his favor. Whether you call him a "mini-Nautica" or just a really annoying bird, he deserves a spot on your roster.
A must-have for tournament enthusiasts. Level him, emblem him, and watch your defense grade soar.
